titleStmt.title: SII 38.243: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 243. (A.R. No. 243 of 1912.) TIRUVOṞṞIYŪR, SAIDAPET TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT. On the gōpura, left of entrance, Ādhipurīśvara temple.

p.hi: King: Vijayagaṇḍagōpāla Year: 21, Siṁha, śu. di. 5. Wednesday, Sōdi = 12th August 1271 A.D.

summary.p: This inscription records the gift of eight cows for burning a quarter-lamp by Umaiyuḍaṉkūḍaniṉṟāṉ Tiruvoṟṟiyūr-piḷḷai, a devotee of the god at Tiruvoṟṟiyūr, which was located in Puḻar-kōṭṭam alias Vikramachōḷa-vaḷanāḍu (a sub-division) of Jayaṅkoṇḍachōḷa-maṇḍalam. Siṅgakōṉ Kaṇṇaṉ, a shepherd of the street (called) Tribhuvanasundara-perunteruvu received the sheep and agreed to measure twentytwo nāḻi of ghee perpetually for the above purpose.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tiripuvaṉacakkaravattikaḷ [śrī]vijayakaṇṭa kopāla
tevaṟku yāṇṭu 2 10 1 vatu siṁhanāyaṟṟu pūrvvapakṣattu pañcami
yum putaṉ kiḻamaiyum peṟṟa cotināḷ jayaṅkoṇṭacoḻamaṇṭala
ttu puḻaṟkoṭṭamāṉa vikramacoḻavaḷanāṭṭu Uṭaiyārtiruvo
ṟṟiyūr Uṭaiya nThe letter ‘na’ is found engraved like taĀyaṉāṟku Ikkoyil tiruviti Āṇṭārkaḷil
Umaiyuṭaṉ kūṭaniṉṟāṉ tiruvoṟṟiyūrppiḷḷaiyeṉ Iṭṭa tiru
nuttāRead nuntāviḷakku kālukku viṭṭa Uru 8 Ippacu Eṭṭum kaikkoṇṭeṉ
tiripuvaṉacuntarapThe letter ‘p’ is found engraved below the line.peruṉteruvil maṉṟāṭikaḷil ciṅkakkoṉ kaṇṇaṉe
n Iviḷakku kālum kaikoṇṭu Āṇṭuoṉṟukku Aḷakkum cilavu nā
ḻiyāl ney 2 10 2 m Inneyi Irupattu Irunāḻi Uriyum nāṉum Eṉ
cantāṉattārum cantirātittavarai Aḷakka kaṭaveṉ ciṅkakkoṉ
kaṇṇaneṉ Ivai kaṇṇakoṉ Eḻuttu śrī māheścararakṣai §
Digital edition of SII 38.243 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
